title: Evaluate snapshot-based indexd search outside the shared store lock
goal: Move indexd exact-search ranking and snippet materialization outside the shared mutable store lock by taking a bounded immutable namespace snapshot, while preserving one-request consistency, deterministic ranking, persistence compatibility and measured memory limits.

acceptance.assertion: The read-lock is held only long enough to validate dimensions and clone immutable Arc-backed references for the selected namespace; no embedding vectors or metadata values are deep-cloned per search.
Dot-product ranking, deterministic tie ordering and snippet materialization execute after the shared store read-lock has been released.
Each search observes one internally consistent namespace snapshot even when concurrent upsert or delete operations replace live entries after snapshot capture.
Public search errors, dimensionality behavior, zero-query ordering, result scores, snippets and JSONL persistence remain compatible with the pre-change implementation.

The commit-bound real-workload benchmark shows a material writer lock-wait reduction on medium and large standard scenarios; search latency and allocation changes are reported and any regression budget breach blocks merge.

Per-search snapshot memory is O(namespace entries) pointer/reference storage rather than O(entries × dimensions), with an explicit report of allocation cost and no unbounded retained snapshot registry.
Focused concurrency and persistence regressions, full workspace tests, Clippy, benchmark contract checks and exact-head GitHub checks pass before merge.
The task does not establish production capacity, ANN readiness, lock-free writes, or correctness beyond the tested exact-search and persistence contracts.

does_not_establish: permission to weaken deterministic exact ranking or persistence semantics
metadata.design_boundary: Arc-backed immutable entries and request-local namespace snapshots; no global snapshot registry and no full vector clone per search.
